parsed_review.summary:
CP00-366 is a descriptor-only RP12 (Billing And Invoicing) "workflow permission slice" closeout pack: 40 units, Risk B, range RP12.P01.M04.S07-RP12.P01.M06.S04. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported and the descriptor-only boundary holds.

Coverage: 40 units with deliverable distribution implementation 22 / ui 6 / fixture 2 / test 6 / hermes_evidence 2 / claude_review 2 and micro distribution M04 14 / M05 22 / M06 4 are mutually consistent across the review baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json (CP00-366 entry), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contract cp366_requirements, and the registry constants. The M04 split is correct: CP00-365 covered M04.S01-S06 (package directory layout..ownership metadata) and CP00-366 covers the M04 tail S07-S20 (reference relationship map..closeout handoff), the full M05 model cycle (22 rows), and the M06 head (4 rows).

Implementation (packages/billing/src registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js) is pure descriptor generation returning frozen objects; grep found no writeFile/exec/spawn/fetch/network/process.env usage. Every result carries no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, synthetic_only:true, loads_real_fixture_data:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, and per-row guards enforce descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no cross-tenant access, no state-transition writes, customer-safe errors, no raw payload, and claude_final_approval_claimed:false.

Contract billing-core-contract.json is internally consistent (current_pack=CP00-366, workflow_permission_slice_descriptor=BillingCoreCp366WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor, cp366_requirements/attestations present, historical_pack_bindings tail=CP00-366); scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs deepEquals the contract projection against the generated constants and asserts production_ready_candidate plus read-only/no-final-approval boundaries. test/model.test.js exercises CP364/365/366 coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, and authority boundaries.

Authority boundary intact: review-request.json sets read_only:true with tools Read,Grep,Glob; descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; manifest claude_not_final_approver=true. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review evidence/receipts (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false), not product runtime.

No overclaim: manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review; enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true; the Hermes "production_ready_candidate" is appropriately labeled a candidate gated on downstream human approval.

Review-hardening preconditions met: readiness-report auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, non-zero stdout), prompt 2236B is within the 25600B limit and excludes full diff/source (source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used), forbidden_tools_present=false, review schema present, summary_only prohibited for Risk B. Downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) and raw-output/review-receipt are not yet present, which is expected because this review run produces them. The unrelated dirty/untracked files (workbook/*, LDIP invalid-attempt json, etc.) are correctly classified as not part of this pack and were not treated as in-scope. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic observation on inherited attestation flags. Final enterprise-trust approval remains a human responsibility outside this receipt.

findings.title: CP00-366 no-write attestation carries inherited descriptor-only validation flags from upstream packs
findings.details: BILLING_CORE_CP366_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is built by spreading BILLING_CORE_CP365_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (which spreads CP364), so the CP00-366 attestation retains validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true and validates_model_foundation_slice_descriptor_only:true alongside its own validates_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_only:true. For a pack whose scope is the workflow permission slice, the first two flags read as inherited markers rather than this pack's own scope. The contract mirrors this exactly at contracts/billing-core-contract.json cp366_no_write_attestation (line 616) and no_write_attestation (line 639), so the rp12:billing-core:validate deepEqual passes. These are additive boolean attestations (all true) and do not weaken any gate, open any runtime, or make a false production_ready / enterprise-trust claim.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this pack. The registry constant and the contract projection must remain in lockstep (changing one alone would break the validator deepEqual). If clearer per-pack semantics are desired, scope these flags in a future closeout-pack generator change rather than hand-editing the contract.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking, informational only. Does not affect the verdict, pack closeout, or goal closeout; no fix required before commit.
